D: Relaxed tension lines in skin – This sounds like it relates to the natural relaxation state of skin layers, maybe indicating directions where the skin can stretch without resistance.

If certain parts of the skin have aligned collagen fibers, those would be weaker points when stretched beyond their natural direction. In cases like hanging, the force applied would follow these pre-existing lines of least resistance, leading to characteristic injury patterns.

Therefore, D remains accurate because it focuses on the intrinsic properties of the skin rather than externally imposed conditions. That aligns with the idea that these lines exist regardless of any trauma and influence how damage occurs when stress is applied.

Therefore, after considering all options and cross-referencing with known anatomical information regarding collagen structure and its role in determining wound propagation tendencies, I concur with selecting D as the appropriate description for Kraissl's lines.
